# Utilitarianism

Utilitarianism is an ethical theory holding that the right action is the one that
maximizes overall happiness or well-being. It was developed by Jeremy Bentham and
later refined by John Stuart Mill in the nineteenth century. Bentham proposed
measuring pleasure and pain to compare outcomes. Mill distinguished higher
pleasures of the intellect from lower bodily pleasures. Utilitarianism is a form
of consequentialism, judging actions by their results rather than intentions.
